interactions.summary: Jiang closes by acknowledging that Trump could pick someone else, especially J.D. Vance, and that Trump could lose. He frames being wrong as useful because the analyst can revise the model, then previews the next class on Iran if a Haley vice presidency pushes toward war.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Likely Jiang closing the class lecture.

speaker_notes.note: Both focus refs are diarized as SPEAKER_01 and read as Jiang's concluding methodological statement and preview.

claims.claim: Jiang acknowledges that Trump might pick someone other than Haley in August, naming J.D. Vance as a plausible loyal-to-Trump alternative.
claims.temporal_scope: Forward-looking uncertainty about the 2024 Republican VP pick.

claims.claim: Jiang says he still bets that Trump will pick Haley, even while admitting there is also a good chance of a J.D. Vance pick or another choice.
claims.temporal_scope: Dated prediction and uncertainty stated on 2024-05-17.

claims.claim: Jiang says that if Trump does not pick Haley or loses in November, the proper response is to return to the analysis and change the model.
claims.temporal_scope: Method statement tied to future 2024 outcomes.

claims.claim: Jiang defines critical thinking as building analytical models, testing them against reality, and refining them based on what reality shows.
Jiang says that if Haley becomes vice president, she will likely agitate for war against Iran and Trump will go along, making a future Iran-war analysis necessary.
claims.temporal_scope: Conditional future prediction about a Trump-Haley administration.

signature_moments.moment: Wrong predictions are not embarrassments; they are what force the model to improve.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I go back to this analysis and I make changes to this analysis
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This rescues the episode from simple punditry by making prediction a method of learning.

signature_moments.moment: Critical thinking is a model tested by reality and refined by reality.
signature_moments.source_phrase: building analytical models that we can then test against reality
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the lecture's explicit methodological thesis and should survive in the public read.

signature_moments.moment: The Haley VP prediction points forward to an Iran-war curriculum.
signature_moments.source_phrase: if Nikki Haley becomes the vice president, it is very likely she will agitate for war against Iran
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This ties the electoral prediction to Jiang's broader geopolitical forecast.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's named alternative VP possibility, characterized chiefly by loyalty to Trump.
The method of revising analysis after predictions meet reality.
Jiang's criterion for whether an analytical model works.

chronology_notes.note: The closing explicitly names possible future falsifiers for the May 2024 prediction: Trump could pick J.D. Vance or someone else, or Trump could lose in November.
